summary: Iteration 14 — Full backend + frontend testing of Employee Exit Management system. Backend: 50/53 tests pass (94%), 3 skipped. Frontend: All key UI flows verified including detail panel, 4 tabs, NOC sections, documents, mobile layout.

minor.issue: No validation prevents submitting a new exit for an already-exited employee (status='exited'). After exit completion, admin can create duplicate exits for the same employee. Currently 2 completed exits exist for RMF0007 as a result of test runs.
Approval chain can include exited/inactive employees as level-1 approvers (e.g., RMF0001 is level-1 for RMF0009). These approvals can never be completed since the approver's account is disabled.

issues: Admin always sees '+ Resign' button, even though admin has no employee_id. Clicking without providing employee override gives a 400 error. Consider renaming to 'Submit Resignation' or making intent clearer for admin vs employee roles.

submit_resignation_api.details: POST /api/exit with multipart form data works. Duplicate active exit blocked (400). Missing reason returns 422.

get_exit_list_role_based.details: Admin sees all exits. Employee (RMF0007) sees only own exits. Status filter works (submitted/completed/rejected). Unauthenticated returns 403.

noc_sections_endpoint.details: GET /api/exit/noc-sections returns 5 sections (branch_manager/accounts/it/audit/admin) with correct item counts (7/3/3/2/3) and labels.

approval_flow_manager.details: Level-1 approval via manager works. Admin cannot bypass manager approval (403). Manager approval keeps status='submitted'. Note: RMF0003 login failing with all known passwords — management accounts (RMF0002/RMF0003/RMF0004) do NOT accept Radhya@123. Level-1 approval was simulated via DB for test isolation.

approval_flow_admin_with_lwd.details: Admin final approval requires last_working_day (400 without it). With LWD, status transitions to noc_in_progress. Employee status updated to notice_period in employees collection.

noc_5_sections_all_cleared.details: All 5 NOC sections (branch_manager/accounts/it/audit/admin) submitted via admin. After 5th section: status=noc_complete, all_cleared=True. Invalid section returns 400. NOC on submitted exit returns 400.

final_docs_upload.details: F&F sheet + Relieving letter uploaded. After both: status=completed, employee status=exited, user account deactivated. Binary data NOT exposed in list API response.

document_download.details: Download fnf_sheet and relieving_letter work. Invalid doc type returns 400. Non-existent doc returns 404.

lwd_update.details: PUT /api/exit/{id}/lwd updates LWD and persists. Non-admin returns 403. LWD update on submitted exit returns 400.

lwd_login_disable.details: RMF0001 (exited employee) login returns 403 with 'exited' in error message. Auto-disable on past LWD implemented in auth.py login endpoint.

ui_exit_page_load.details: Exit Management page loads. Stats cards show 4 metrics (Total/Pending/NOC/Done). Filter buttons work. Search works.

ui_detail_panel_tabs.details: Clicking exit card opens full-screen detail panel. 4 tabs: Overview (employee info + timeline), Approvals (level-by-level chain), NOC (5/5 cleared sections with items), Documents (F&F estimate + files). All tabs functional.

ui_resign_modal.details: Resign button opens modal with: Date of Resignation, Reason textarea, optional file upload, Employee ID override (admin only), notice period info, Cancel/Submit buttons.

ui_mobile_layout.details: 390px mobile view: 4-column stats grid, stacked cards, filter buttons scrollable, detail panel with bottom-slide style, NOC tab shows all 5 sections with checkmarks per item. Bottom tab navigation visible.

ffs_endpoint.details: GET /api/exit/{id}/ffs returns EL encashment, gratuity eligibility, total amount, years of service.

action_items: MEDIUM: Add validation in POST /api/exit to block submission for employees with status='exited'. Currently admin can create new exits for already-exited employees, causing duplicate exit records.

MEDIUM: Review approval chain building — consider skipping exited/inactive employees when building the approval chain in _build_approval_chain(). RMF0009's exit has level-1 approver RMF0001 (exited), which can never be approved.

MINOR: RMF0003's login is failing with Radhya@123 and other common passwords. HR should reset or document RMF0003's password. This affects the approval workflow for employees who report to RMF0003 (RMF0005, RMF0007, RMF0012).

CLEANUP: Test artifacts left in DB — RMF0009 (id: 6a2ba5b14d78dc71552c0c38) and RMF0023 (id: 6a2ba5d64d78dc71552c0c39) have submitted exits that show as 'Action needed' for admin. Also 2 completed exits for RMF0007. Consider adding a cleanup script or reject/delete endpoint for admin.

MINOR UX: Admin's Resign button is always visible. If admin clicks without specifying an employee ID override, they get a 400 error. Consider disabling the button or showing a tooltip if no employee ID is provided.
critical_code_review_comments: exit_routes.py submit_resignation: No check for emp.get('status') == 'exited'. An admin can submit a new resignation for an already-exited employee (e.g., after their exit was completed). Add: if emp.get('status') == 'exited': raise HTTPException(400, 'Cannot submit resignation for an exited employee').

_build_approval_chain: Builds chain from reporting_to hierarchy without checking if the approver's account is active. If a reporting manager has exited (e.g., RMF0001), they become a permanent blocker in the chain since they can't log in to approve. Consider checking user account status or adding an admin-override approval for cases like this.

exit_routes.py approve_exit: The action field is not validated (only 'approve' and 'reject' are valid). If an invalid action like 'pending' is passed, it would set pending_item['status'] to 'pending' which could cause logical issues. Add: if data.action not in ['approve', 'reject']: raise HTTPException(422, 'Action must be approve or reject').

ExitManagement.js: The +Resign button should ideally be hidden or renamed for admin role, since admin submits 'on behalf'. Currently can lead to a confusing 400 error if admin clicks without specifying an employee.

success_rate.backend: 94% (50/53 passed, 3 skipped — all skips are valid/non-critical)
success_rate.frontend: 95% (all major flows verified, 1 minor UX design note)

test_credentials.employee: RMF0007 / Radhya@123 (account now inactive after test exit completion)
test_credentials.manager_rmf0003: RMF0003 / UNKNOWN — does not accept Radhya@123

seed_data_creation: Test created 2 exit requests for RMF0007 (both now completed), 1 for RMF0009 (submitted - test artifact), 1 for RMF0023 (submitted - test artifact). RMF0007 user account is now is_active=False due to exit completion. Test also manually modified DB to simulate level-1 approval for RMF0007's first exit (RMF0003's approval was simulated).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Exit Management system is fully functional and tested. Backend 50/53 passing. Key test data: RMF0007 has 2 completed exits (test artifacts). RMF0009 and RMF0023 have pending 'submitted' exits (test artifacts). RMF0001 is exited. RMF0007 user account is_active=False. RMF0003 cannot login with Radhya@123. For testing manager approval flows, use RMF0010 (login works) as approver for employees who report to them (e.g., RMF0017). Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_exit_management.py

duplicate_exits_for_rmf0007.description: 2 completed exits exist for RMF0007. Caused by multiple test runs — after first exit was completed, the duplicate check (only filters non-completed exits) allowed a new one to be created.
duplicate_exits_for_rmf0007.root_cause: submit_resignation endpoint only blocks active (non-rejected, non-completed) exits. Once completed, a new exit can be submitted for the same employee, even if employee.status='exited'.

rmf0003_login_failure.description: RMF0003 cannot login with Radhya@123 or Admin@12345
rmf0003_login_failure.root_cause: Management accounts (RMF0002/RMF0003/RMF0004) were possibly created with a custom password before the Feb 2026 bulk reset, and may not have been included in the reset. Their must_change_password=False, so they haven't been flagged for reset.
rmf0003_login_failure.mitigation: Admin should reset via POST /api/auth/employees/RMF0003/reset-password. Or document the correct password in test_credentials.md.
